modelDescription.nano: Tiny and quick — barely uses memory.
modelDescription.mini: Light and snappy for everyday questions.

modelDescription.standard: Balanced capability with a moderate footprint.
modelDescription.pro: Capable enough for complex, multi-step work.
modelDescription.ultra: Highly capable — needs plenty of memory.
modelDescription.extreme: The biggest weights this family ships.

modelPicker.catalogUnavailable: Couldn't reach Ollama's registry. Check your connection and try again.

pullHints.lookingUp: Finding your model in Ollama's registry.

pullErrors.manifestMissing: Model unavailable — pick another or update Ollama.

modelPicker.availableDescription: Models already downloaded on your machine, ready to use.

modelPicker.noAvailableModels: No models found in this folder.

wolffish.title: Wolffish
wolffish.subtitle: Runtime behavior. Off by default — turn things on as you trust them.

launchAtStartup.label: Launch at startup
launchAtStartup.description: Automatically start Wolffish when you log into your computer. When enabled, Wolffish registers itself as a login item with your operating system — macOS, Windows, and Linux are all supported. This lets Wolffish be ready the moment your session begins, so you can jump straight into a conversation without opening it manually. Disable this if you prefer to launch Wolffish only when you need it.

blockCredentials.label: Block sensitive data in messages
blockCredentials.description: When enabled, messages that appear to contain passwords, API keys, tokens, or private keys are immediately discarded — they never reach the agent, are never stored, and you get a short notification instead. Off by default so the agent can freely discuss credentials when needed. Turn it on if you want a hard guard against accidentally pasting secrets into chat.

bypassPermissions.label: Bypass permissions mode
bypassPermissions.description: When enabled, all tool actions auto-approve without showing the approval dialog. The amygdala still classifies every action and logs everything to the corpus event bus and feedback files exactly as before — only the human approval step is skipped. Use this for trusted environments where you want full agent autonomy. Disable it any time to restore manual approval.

allowLocalFallback.label: Run agentic tasks on the local model
allowLocalFallback.description: Your local model always answers and chats. This switch decides whether it also gets Wolffish's full toolset — running commands, editing files, and chaining steps the way cloud models do. Off keeps it in plain conversation mode and declines tool-heavy requests. Recommended off — smaller local models tend to misuse tools, loop, or invent results. Turn it on only if your local model is large enough to handle multi-step tool use reliably.

restrictPowerfulModels.label: Restrict powerful local models
restrictPowerfulModels.description: When enabled, the model picker blocks local models whose memory footprint exceeds what your system can handle comfortably (~55% of total RAM). This prevents severe slowdowns, swap thrashing, and system instability caused by loading oversized models. Turning this off lets you install and run any model regardless of hardware limits — not recommended. Oversized models force your system into heavy disk swapping, making both Wolffish and your entire machine unresponsive.

showChatAnalytics.label: Show in-chat analytics
showChatAnalytics.description: Display a small strip next to the chat input showing elapsed turn time, output tokens, and context size. Off by default — turn it on if you want live visibility into model usage and timing.

weekStartsOn.label: Start of week
weekStartsOn.description: Sets the day Wolffish treats as the first day of the week. Drives how the activity heatmap is laid out and how date ranges like "this week" line up in your head. Default is Monday (the ISO 8601 standard most of the world uses); pick Sunday if your calendar app and routines start there. Internal weekly memory files keep their existing ISO-week names so older summaries don't shift.

compaction.title: Memory compaction
compaction.subtitle: Wolffish compacts your conversations into long-term memory. Daily compaction extracts key facts from each day's episodes and promotes them to knowledge files. Weekly consolidation merges the week's episodes into a single digest.

daily.label: Daily compaction
daily.description: Extracts key facts from today's conversations and saves them to knowledge files.

weekly.label: Weekly consolidation
weekly.description: Merges the week's episode logs into a single digest for archival.

cellebrum.title: Cellebrum
cellebrum.subtitle: Loaded capabilities and their runtime status.

descriptions.stepfun: Step — frontier reasoning models with built-in thinking and vision support
descriptions.qwen: Alibaba's Qwen models — powerful reasoning and coding with long-context support
descriptions.xai: Grok — xAI's frontier reasoning models with vision and live web search
descriptions.minimax: MiniMax's frontier models — strong reasoning and code with competitive pricing
descriptions.kimi: Moonshot AI's flagship models — powerful reasoning with long-context support
descriptions.mimo: Xiaomi's cloud AI — affordable frontier models with strong multilingual support
descriptions.deepseek: Open-weight reasoning powerhouse — frontier performance at a fraction of the cost
descriptions.anthropic: Claude — safety-focused frontier models with exceptional long-context reasoning
descriptions.openai: GPT and o-series — the industry-standard frontier model family
descriptions.openrouter: Unified API gateway — access models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, and more with a single key
descriptions.ollama: Open-weight models running locally — private, offline, no API key needed

tts.title: Text-to-Speech
tts.subtitle: Configure how Wolffish generates spoken audio from text.
tts.engineDescription: Neural text-to-speech. No API key required.
tts.previewHint: Preview uses your browser's built-in voices

stt.title: Speech-to-Text
stt.subtitle: Configure how Wolffish transcribes audio into text.
stt.engineDescription: Open-source speech recognition. Runs entirely on your machine — no API key, no cloud.
stt.languageDescription: Auto-detected by default. You can specify a language per transcription to improve accuracy.

telegram.title: Telegram
telegram.subtitle: Talk to Wolffish from Telegram. Same agent, same memory, same history.
telegram.enableDescription: Message Wolffish from Telegram to access it remotely.
telegram.botTokenHint: Get one from @BotFather on Telegram. Treated as a secret — stored in config.json on your machine.
telegram.allowedUsersHint: Send /start to @userinfobot to get your ID.
telegram.testHint: Sends a verification message to the first allowed user without taking the bot online.

validation.tokenRequired: Bot token is required when enabled.
validation.userIdRequired: Add at least one user ID when enabled.

errors.invalid_token: Token rejected — copy a fresh one from @BotFather.
errors.invalid_user_id: Invalid user ID — get yours from @userinfobot.
errors.rate_limit: Rate-limited — wait a minute and retry.

autoRefresh.label: Auto-refresh conversations
autoRefresh.description: Start a fresh conversation after idle time. Previous ones stay accessible via /resume.

telegram.commandsSubtitle: Slash commands users send from Telegram. Type each one exactly.

commands.new: Start a fresh conversation. Only works when nothing is processing — use /stop first if a task is running.
commands.resume: Pick a past conversation to continue. The chat switches to the one you choose.
commands.delete: Pick a conversation to delete. If it was the active one, a fresh conversation starts.
commands.stop: Cancel the task currently running in this chat.
commands.approve: Approve a pending confirmation prompt (e.g. before a destructive action runs).

limitations.singleConversation: Only one conversation processes at a time.
limitations.busyNotQueued: Messages sent while busy aren't queued — resend after.
limitations.allowList: Only allowed user IDs receive replies.

brave.title: Brave Search
brave.subtitle: Use Brave Search API as the primary web-search provider. When enabled, the agent's web_search tool calls Brave first and only falls back to DuckDuckGo if Brave is unreachable or rate-limited.
brave.enableDescription: When on, web searches go through Brave first. When off, DuckDuckGo is used directly.
brave.apiKeyHint: Get a key from api.search.brave.com (free tier: 2,000 queries/month). Stored in config.json on your machine — never sent anywhere except api.search.brave.com.
brave.testHint: Sends a tiny query to Brave to verify the key works. Counts toward your monthly quota.
brave.testSuccess: Brave API key works ({{count}} results returned)

validation.keyRequired: API key is required when enabled.

errors.invalid_key: Key rejected — copy a new one from Brave.
errors.rate_limit: Rate-limited by Brave — wait and retry.
errors.subscription: Brave subscription expired — check your plan.

howItWorks.primary: Brave runs first when enabled with a valid key.
howItWorks.fallback: Falls back to DuckDuckGo on rate-limit, error, or timeout.
howItWorks.paid: Heavier use needs a paid plan.
howItWorks.privacy: Key stays in your config.json — no proxy, no telemetry.

notion.title: Notion
notion.subtitle: Connect to Notion to read and write pages, databases, and blocks. The agent uses your integration token to access workspaces shared with the integration.
notion.tokenHint: Create an internal integration at notion.so/my-integrations, then share pages/databases with it. Token is stored in config.json on your machine — never sent anywhere except api.notion.com.
notion.testHint: Calls Notion’s /v1/users/me endpoint to verify the integration token is valid.

errors.invalid_token: Token rejected — copy a fresh one from Notion.
errors.rate_limit: Rate-limited by Notion — wait and retry.

howItWorks.integration: Create an internal integration at notion.so/my-integrations.
howItWorks.pages: Share specific pages or databases with the integration to grant access.
howItWorks.databases: The agent can search, read, create, and update pages and databases.
howItWorks.privacy: Token stays in your config.json — no proxy, no telemetry.
howItWorks.setup: Only pages explicitly shared with the integration are accessible.

github.title: GitHub
github.subtitle: Connect to GitHub to manage repos, issues, pull requests, branches, CI/Actions, releases, and gists. The agent uses your Personal Access Token to authenticate API calls.
github.tokenHint: Create a token at <link>github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens</link> with the 'repo' scope (and 'gist' for gists). Token is stored in config.json on your machine — never sent anywhere except api.github.com.
github.testHint: Calls GitHub's /user endpoint to verify the token is valid and shows your username.

errors.invalid_token: Token rejected — copy a fresh one from GitHub.
errors.rate_limit: Rate-limited by GitHub — wait and retry.

howItWorks.pat: Create a Personal Access Token at <link>github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens</link>.
howItWorks.scopes: Grant 'repo' scope for full repository access, 'gist' for gists.
howItWorks.tools: The agent can list repos, manage issues and PRs, check CI, create releases, and search code.
howItWorks.privacy: Token stays in your config.json — no proxy, no telemetry.
howItWorks.ci: CI/Actions logs are available for debugging failed workflows.

github.capabilitiesTitle: What you can ask Wolffish to do
github.capabilitiesSubtitle: Destructive actions are confirmed before they run.

capabilities.repos: Browse your repositories, get repo details, or create new ones.
capabilities.issues: Read, create, and close issues — including comments and labels.
capabilities.prs: Open pull requests, review their status, and merge them (with approval).
capabilities.branches: List branches and clean up merged ones (with approval).
capabilities.ci: Check CI/Actions runs and pull workflow logs to debug failures.
capabilities.releases: Cut a release with tag, notes, draft, and prerelease support (with approval).
capabilities.search: Search code across all of GitHub by keyword, filename, or language.
capabilities.files: Read any file from any accessible repo without cloning it locally.
capabilities.gists: List your gists or create a new one to share a snippet.

google.title: Google Workspace
google.subtitle: Gmail, Drive, Calendar, Contacts, Tasks, and Sheets — drop your OAuth JSON to connect.

setup.label: gogcli: Google Suite CLI
setup.needsInstall: Click Install — Wolffish will download gogcli for you

credentials.label: OAuth Credentials
credentials.hint: OAuth Desktop client JSON from console.cloud.google.com → APIs & Services → Credentials.
credentials.dropzone: Drop OAuth client JSON here or click to browse
credentials.rotate: Drop a new JSON to replace credentials
credentials.guidePrereq: You need a Google account — any free Gmail works.
credentials.step1Rest: and create a new project (name it anything)
credentials.step1Confirm: You see the project dashboard with your project name in the top bar.
credentials.step2: Go to APIs & Services → Library and search for each API below — click it then hit "Enable"
credentials.step2Confirm: Each enabled API appears in the "Enabled APIs & services" dashboard.
credentials.step3: Go to OAuth consent screen → set User type to "External" → fill in your email → add yourself as a Test user
credentials.step3Confirm: You see the consent screen summary with status "Testing".
credentials.step4: Go to Credentials → Create Credentials → OAuth client ID → choose "Desktop app"
credentials.step4Confirm: The new client appears in the OAuth 2.0 Client IDs list.
credentials.step5: Click the download icon (⬇) next to the client to get the JSON file
credentials.step6: Drop the JSON file below — Wolffish takes it from here
credentials.step6Confirm: The credentials section shows a green checkmark after upload.
credentials.guideTip1: Leave publishing status as "Testing" — only test users can authorize, which is fine for personal use.
credentials.guideTip2: You can always enable more APIs later — just go back to APIs & Services → Library.

auth.label: Accounts
auth.hint: Add multiple accounts — the LLM picks which one to use on every call, and fans out across all of them for identity-agnostic requests.

capabilities.title: Available services
list: Gmail — search, send, attachments, labels, drafts, filters, auto-reply, vacation
Calendar — list/create/update events, invites, OOO, working-location, free/busy
Drive — list, search, upload, download, convert, permissions, shared drives
Docs — create, edit by tab, import Markdown, find/replace, export
Sheets — read/write ranges, rows/cols, tabs, formatting, find/replace
Slides — create, copy, export, generate from Markdown or templates
Contacts — search, create, update, directory, custom fields
Tasks — manage tasklists and tasks with recurring schedules
Forms — create, manage questions, inspect responses
Chat — spaces, messages, DMs, emoji reactions (Workspace)
Classroom — courses, roster, coursework, submissions, announcements
Apps Script — create projects, inspect content, run functions
Keep — list, search, create, delete notes (Workspace)
Multiple accounts — switch between Google accounts with aliases
Encrypted backups — export account data to age-encrypted Git shards
Email tracking — track opens for sent mail

memes.title: Memes
memes.subtitle: Generate captioned memes and search for reaction GIFs. Memegen.link works out of the box — Giphy and Imgflip need credentials.

memegen.title: Memegen
memegen.description: Free, zero-config meme generator. Always available — no account needed.

giphy.title: Giphy
giphy.description: Search and download reaction GIFs. Requires a free API key.
giphy.hint: Sign up at developers.giphy.com, create an app, and copy the API key.

imgflip.title: Imgflip
imgflip.description: Premium meme generation with more templates. Requires a free account.
imgflip.hint: Create a free account at imgflip.com. Username and password are used to generate memes.

whatsapp.title: WhatsApp
whatsapp.subtitle: Connect Wolffish to WhatsApp. Scan the QR code to link your account.
whatsapp.enableDescription: Connect to WhatsApp and listen for messages.
whatsapp.connectDescription: Link your WhatsApp account to get started.
whatsapp.scanQr: Scan this QR code with WhatsApp on your phone.
whatsapp.scanQrHelp: Open WhatsApp → Settings → Linked Devices → Link a Device
whatsapp.allowedPhonesHint: Comma-separated phone numbers. Only messages from these numbers will be processed.
whatsapp.commandsSubtitle: Send these commands in your WhatsApp chat.

limitations.singleConversation: One active conversation per phone number at a time
limitations.busyNotQueued: Messages sent while busy get a short reply, not queued
limitations.allowList: Only allowed phone numbers can interact
limitations.silentIgnore: Messages from non-allowed numbers are silently ignored

validation.phoneRequired: At least one phone number is required when enabled

computerUse.title: Computer Use
computerUse.subtitle: Let the agent see your screen and control your mouse and keyboard to perform desktop tasks.
computerUse.enableHint: When enabled, the agent can take screenshots, move the mouse, click, scroll, and type.
computerUse.resolutionHint: Maximum width in pixels. Lower values use less tokens but reduce detail.
computerUse.formatHint: JPEG is smaller and faster. PNG is lossless and better for text-heavy screens.
computerUse.saveError: Failed to save Computer Use settings

howItWorks.step1: The agent takes a screenshot to see what's on your screen.
howItWorks.step2: It analyzes the image and decides the next action (click, type, scroll, etc.).
howItWorks.step3: After each action it takes another screenshot to verify the result.
howItWorks.step4: This loop continues until the task is complete.

browserExtension.title: Browser Extension
browserExtension.subtitle: Connect the Wolffish browser extension to control Chrome or Brave from the agent.
browserExtension.portHint: The port the extension connects to. Default is 23151. Change only if there's a conflict.
browserExtension.resolutionHint: Maximum width in pixels. Lower values use less tokens but reduce detail.
browserExtension.formatHint: JPEG is smaller and faster. PNG is lossless and better for text-heavy screens.
browserExtension.step3: Click "Load unpacked" and select — or drag & drop — the extension folder below
browserExtension.step4: The extension will connect automatically to Wolffish
browserExtension.waitingForConnection: Server is running — install the extension and it will connect automatically.
browserExtension.actionsSubtitle: 46 tools the agent can use when the extension is connected.

actions.ext_read_page: Extract page content as text, markdown, or HTML
actions.ext_query_selector: Query DOM elements by CSS selector
actions.ext_get_url: Get the current URL and title
actions.ext_screenshot: Screenshot the page or an element
actions.ext_download: Download a file from a URL
actions.ext_wait_for: Wait for an element to appear
actions.ext_debugger_attach: Attach debugger for trusted input events

debuggerMode.title: Debugger Mode
debuggerMode.body: Wolffish uses Chrome's debugger API to simulate human-like browser interactions (clicks, typing, scrolling) that are indistinguishable from real user input. This works automatically on all Chromium-based browsers — Chrome, Brave, Edge, Arc, Opera, and Vivaldi. No setup is required.
debuggerMode.infobarNote: When debugger mode is active, a yellow infobar saying "Extension is debugging this page" may appear at the top of the tab. This is a standard Chrome notification and is purely cosmetic — it does not affect functionality. To hide it, launch your browser with the following flag:
debuggerMode.tipText: Create a separate browser shortcut with this flag for Wolffish automation sessions.
debuggerMode.notSupportedText: Firefox and Safari do not support the debugger extension API. Debugger mode is Chromium-only.

variables.title: Variables
variables.subtitle: Store API keys, tokens, base URLs, and other values in one place.
variables.empty: No variables yet. Add one to get started.
variables.duplicate: A variable with this name already exists.
variables.hint: Variables are stored in config.json and injected into every conversation. The agent can read them to authenticate API calls, connect to services, or fill in values you'd otherwise have to paste each time. Sensitive variables are masked in the UI but available to the agent in plaintext.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.subtitle: Customize how Wolffish looks and reads.

usage.title: Usage
usage.subtitle: Token usage and cost across providers.

data.title: Data
data.subtitle: How much space Wolffish is using and how to start over.

factoryReset.label: Factory reset
factoryReset.description: Deletes all memories, conversations, feedback, task logs, event logs, debug snapshots, knowledge files, and usage data. Default workspace files are recreated from bundled templates. Your API keys, model selection, language, and theme are preserved so you don't have to re-enter them.
factoryReset.title: Factory reset Wolffish?
factoryReset.warning: This wipes every memory, conversation, and log. API keys, model selection, language, and theme are preserved. The app will restart. This cannot be undone.

updates.title: Updates
updates.subtitle: Manage app updates and version info.
updates.autoUpdatesDescription: Check for and download updates automatically on launch.

empty.title: Wolffish is ready
empty.subtitle: Ask anything. I bite through anything.

noModel.notice: No local model or cloud provider configured.

voice.permissionDenied: Mic access denied — allow in system settings.

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.subtitle: Browse and edit the files that shape Wolffish's behavior.
workspace.pickFile: Select a file from the tree to view or edit it.
workspace.unsupported: This file type can't be previewed here.
workspace.resetWarning: Your current changes to this file will be replaced with the bundled default. This cannot be undone.

heartbeat.title: Heartbeat
heartbeat.noJobs: No automations active. Add schedule headings to heartbeat.md to get started.

overlay.blocked: Chat is paused while a heartbeat job is running.
overlay.compactionDaily: Summarize today's episodes and promote key facts to long-term knowledge.
overlay.compactionWeekly: Consolidate the past week's episodes into a digest.

history.empty: No conversations yet. Start chatting to see them here.
history.deleteWarning: This conversation will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone.

provider.noProviderDescription: The API provider failed to complete the response — this is not a wolffish issue.
provider.invalidKey: API key invalid or expired — update in settings.
provider.badRequest: The provider rejected the request — try a different model or check your configuration.
provider.modelNotFound: Model not found — may be renamed or retired.
